lede: A look at June 12 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Anne Frank and Emperor Gaozong of Song, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Air India Flight 171
events.desc: Air India Flight 171, a Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner, crashes shortly after takeoff into the B. J. Medical College, Ahmedabad, India, killing 241 out of 242 onboard as well as 19 on the ground. This marked the first fatal crash and hull loss of the Boeing 787 Dreamliner.

events.title: United States
events.desc: United States President Donald Trump and Kim Jong-un of North Korea held the first meeting between leaders of their two countries in Singapore.

events.title: Pulse nightclub shooting
events.desc: Forty-nine civilians are killed and 58 others injured in an attack on a gay nightclub in Orlando, Florida, United States; the gunman, Omar Mateen, is killed in a gunfight with police.

events.title: Cold War
events.desc: Cold War: At the Brandenburg Gate, U.S. President Ronald Reagan publicly challenges Mikhail Gorbachev to tear down the Berlin Wall.

events.title: New York City
events.desc: A nuclear disarmament rally and concert is held in New York City.

events.title: Supreme Court of the United States
events.desc: The United States Supreme Court in Loving v. Virginia declares all U.S. state laws that prohibit interracial marriage to be unconstitutional.

events.title: Cleopatra (1963 film)
events.desc: The film Cleopatra, starring Elizabeth Taylor and Richard Burton, is released in US theaters. It was the most expensive film made at the time.

events.title: Anne Frank
events.desc: Anne Frank receives a diary for her thirteenth birthday.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Thirteen thousand British and French troops surrender to General Erwin Rommel at Saint-Valery-en-Caux.

events.title: Louis IX of France
events.desc: At the instigation of Louis IX of France, an inter-faith debate, known as the Disputation of Paris, starts between a Christian monk and four rabbis.

births.name: Anne Frank
births.line: German-Dutch diarist; victim of the Holocaust

births.name: George H. W. Bush
births.line: American lieutenant and politician, 41st President of the United States

deaths.text: Silvio Berlusconi, Italian businessman and politician, Prime Minister of Italy
Miroslav Dvořák (ice hockey), Czech ice hockey player
Karl von Frisch, Austrian-German ethologist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Hermann Scherchen, German viola player and conductor

observances.text: Christian feast day: 108 Martyrs of World War II
